_comment: DNS records required for AWS SES email (outbound) + Google Workspace (inbound) on myfamousfinds.com
_instructions: Add these records in your DNS provider (Vercel). Vercel auto-appends the domain — use the short 'host' values below, NOT the full FQDN.
_verification_endpoint: GET /api/admin/verify-ses-dns?key=<ADMIN_PASSWORD>  — checks all records via live DNS lookup

_setup_checklist.step_1: Add the 3 DKIM CNAME records in Vercel DNS (see dkim_cname_records below)
_setup_checklist.step_2: Add the MX + TXT records for MAIL FROM subdomain (see mail_from_records below)
_setup_checklist.step_3: Add the DMARC TXT record (see dmarc_record below)
_setup_checklist.step_4: Add the root-domain INBOUND MX + SPF records (see inbound_email_records below)
_setup_checklist.step_5: Wait up to 72 hours for AWS SES to detect the records and flip DKIM from 'Pending' to 'Verified'
_setup_checklist.step_6: Once verified, click 'Request production access' in AWS SES console to leave sandbox mode
_setup_checklist.step_7: Set up SES inbound receiving (see _inbound_email_setup below)
_setup_checklist.step_8: Set AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_SES_FROM, AWS_SES_REPLY_TO in Vercel env vars
_setup_checklist.step_10: Test inbound: send an email to support@myfamousfinds.com and verify it arrives

_inbound_email_setup._why: Without root-domain MX records, emails to *@myfamousfinds.com fall back to the A record (your web server), which does not speak SMTP — causing 'unable to read banner / timed out' bounces.
_inbound_email_setup.step_1: Add the root-domain MX and SPF records below in Vercel DNS (see inbound_email_records)
_inbound_email_setup.step_2_aws_console: In AWS SES > Email receiving > Create rule set, add a receipt rule for support@myfamousfinds.com
_inbound_email_setup.step_3_actions: Add an SNS action that publishes to a topic (e.g. 'myfamousfinds-inbound-email')
_inbound_email_setup.step_4_sns_subscription: Subscribe the SNS topic to your app's webhook: POST https://www.myfamousfinds.com/api/ses/inbound
_inbound_email_setup.step_5_activate: Set the rule set as active in SES
_inbound_email_setup.step_6_env_var: Set SUPPORT_INBOX in Vercel env if you want a quick fallback — replies go to that address instead

dkim_stays_pending: Verify CNAME values end exactly with .dkim.amazonses.com (no trailing dot, no extra text)
Check for domain doubling: host should be 'abc._domainkey' not 'abc._domainkey.myfamousfinds.com' (Vercel auto-appends the domain)
Run the verification endpoint to see which records resolve and which don't
DNS propagation can take up to 72 hours — be patient after corrections
mail_from_stays_pending: Verify MX record: host 'mail' pointing to 'feedback-smtp.us-east-1.amazonses.com' with priority 10
Verify SPF TXT record: host 'mail' with value 'v=spf1 include:amazonses.com ~all'
inbound_bounces: Run: dig MX myfamousfinds.com +short — you should see '10 inbound-smtp.us-east-1.amazonaws.com.'
If you see NO MX result, the root-domain MX record is missing — add it in Vercel DNS
If MX resolves but emails still bounce, check the SES receipt rule set is active and the SNS topic is subscribed
Set SUPPORT_INBOX env var as a quick workaround — replies go to that real inbox

mail_from_records.purpose: MAIL FROM domain for SES bounce handling (outbound)
SPF for the mail subdomain (MAIL FROM alignment)

google_workspace_verification.purpose: Domain ownership verification for Google Workspace

inbound_email_records_ses_legacy.purpose: LEGACY — SES inbound MX. Replaced by Google Workspace MX records above.

root_domain_spf.purpose: Root-domain SPF — authorizes BOTH AWS SES (outbound transactional) and Google Workspace (Gmail) to send on behalf of the domain

dmarc_record.purpose: DMARC policy — monitoring-only (safe to start). Tighten to p=quarantine or p=reject once stable.
